name: Sachiel
description: Sachiel, also spelled Zachiel, is an archangel within Jewish and Christian angelic lore, often associated with the planet Jupiter. His name, derived from the Hebrew roots meaning "covering of God" or "price of God," reflects his role as an intermediary between the divine and the mortal realm. Sachiel is typically invoked for prosperity, benevolence, and justice, reflecting the traditional astrological associations of Jupiter. He is considered one of the ruling princes of the order of angels called the Hashmallim and is sometimes associated with the sephirah Chesed on the Kabbalistic Tree of Life, further emphasizing his connection to mercy and abundance. While specific narratives detailing Sachiel's actions are scarce in canonical religious texts, his significance lies in his symbolic representation of divine grace and his role in influencing worldly affairs, particularly those concerning wealth and leadership. He is often depicted as a wise and compassionate figure, guiding individuals towards ethical and prosperous paths.
subtitle: Angel of Jupiter

keyMyths.title: Sachiel and the King's Prosperity
keyMyths.description: A legendary tale recounts a king burdened with poverty and injustice in his realm. Upon invoking Sachiel's aid, the archangel appeared in a dream, guiding the king towards policies that fostered fairness and equitable distribution of resources. The kingdom flourished under the king's newfound wisdom, proving Sachiel's influence in matters of governance and economic well-being. This myth highlights Sachiel's role as a facilitator of just and prosperous societies.

keyMyths.title: Sachiel's Guidance in Times of Need
keyMyths.description: During a period of famine and hardship, a community turned to prayer, seeking divine intervention. Sachiel, moved by their plight, appeared to a farmer in a vision, revealing a hidden spring of water and teaching him sustainable agricultural practices. The farmer shared his knowledge with the community, leading to their eventual recovery and demonstrating Sachiel's compassion and willingness to aid those in genuine need. This underscores his connection to providing practical solutions and fostering self-sufficiency.

keyMyths.title: The Judgement of Sachiel
keyMyths.description: In a story passed down through generations, Sachiel is said to have intervened in a dispute between two powerful merchants who were exploiting the poor. Seeing their greed and lack of compassion, Sachiel appeared before them, not in a terrifying display of power, but in a gentle reminder of the importance of fairness and ethical conduct. The merchants, humbled by his presence, reformed their ways and contributed to the well-being of their community, illustrating Sachiel's role as a force for moral correction.

sources.author: Davidson, Gustav
